questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is the Airavatesvara Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Airavatesvara Temple stands at Darasuram in the Thanjavur district of Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: To which Hindu deity is the Airavatesvara Temple dedicated?
questions.explanation: It is a Shaiva temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, worshipped here as Airavatesvara.

questions.q: In which town or village is the Airavatesvara Temple situated?
questions.explanation: The temple is located at Darasuram, a small town near Kumbakonam in Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: Which ruling dynasty built the Airavatesvara Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple was built by the Chola dynasty, master temple-builders of medieval Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: In which century was the Airavatesvara Temple built?
questions.explanation: The temple was constructed in the 12th century CE during the later Chola period.

questions.q: The Airavatesvara Temple is famously designed in the form of what?
questions.explanation: The temple's mandapa is shaped like a grand stone chariot, giving it the nickname 'the chariot of Darasuram.'

questions.q: Which large international heritage organization lists the Airavatesvara Temple as a World Heritage Site?
questions.explanation: The temple is inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List as part of the Great Living Chola Temples.

questions.q: The name 'Airavatesvara' is linked to Airavata, the famous white elephant of which deity?
questions.explanation: Airavata is the white elephant of Indra, who according to legend worshipped Shiva here, giving the temple its name.

questions.q: The Airavatesvara Temple is built primarily from which material?
questions.explanation: Like other Chola temples, it is constructed mainly of stone, chiefly granite and finely carved hard stone.

questions.q: The Airavatesvara Temple is grouped under which collective UNESCO heritage title?
options: The Group of Monuments at Hampi
questions.explanation: It is one of the three Great Living Chola Temples recognized together by UNESCO.

questions.q: Which Chola king is credited with building the Airavatesvara Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple was built by Rajaraja Chola II in the 12th century.

questions.q: Darasuram, where the temple stands, lies near which historic town famous for temples and brassware?
questions.explanation: Darasuram is located close to Kumbakonam, a major temple town in the Thanjavur region.

questions.q: Besides Airavatesvara, which other two temples make up the Great Living Chola Temples?
options: Brihadisvara Temple at Thanjavur and Brihadisvara Temple at Gangaikonda Cholapuram
questions.explanation: The trio comprises the Brihadisvara temples at Thanjavur and Gangaikonda Cholapuram along with Airavatesvara at Darasuram.

questions.q: What unique acoustic feature is the Airavatesvara Temple celebrated for?
options: A whispering gallery in the dome
Musical steps that produce sounds when struck
Bells that ring with the wind
questions.explanation: A short flight of stone steps at the temple is said to produce musical notes, earning the name 'singing steps.'

questions.q: In which broad architectural tradition was the Airavatesvara Temple built?
questions.explanation: The temple is a refined example of Dravidian architecture characteristic of the Chola dynasty.

questions.q: Approximately how tall is the main vimana (tower over the sanctum) of the Airavatesvara Temple?
questions.explanation: The vimana rises to roughly 24 metres, more modest in scale than the towering Brihadisvara temples but exquisitely detailed.

questions.q: The temple's front mandapa is sculpted as a chariot drawn by which animals?
questions.explanation: The chariot-shaped mandapa is depicted as being drawn by carved horses with elephants flanking the steps.

questions.q: According to legend reflected in the temple's name, who worshipped Shiva here to be cured of a curse?
questions.explanation: Legend says Airavata, Indra's white elephant, worshipped Shiva here to regain its lost white colour.

questions.q: Which river valley's fertile delta region is the Airavatesvara Temple located in?
questions.explanation: Darasuram lies in the Kaveri delta, the heartland of Chola power in Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: The Airavatesvara Temple was added to the UNESCO World Heritage list as an extension in which year?
questions.explanation: It was added in 2004, extending the Great Living Chola Temples site originally inscribed in 1987.

questions.q: What is the name of the consort goddess shrine associated with the Airavatesvara Temple complex?
questions.explanation: The goddess is worshipped here as Periya Nayaki Amman (Deivanayaki), housed in a separate shrine.

questions.q: The Airavatesvara Temple is renowned for its intricate carvings of dancers and musicians illustrating which art?
questions.explanation: Its panels depict classical dance poses, an important visual record of South Indian dance traditions.

questions.q: Rajaraja Chola II, the temple's builder, was the son of which Chola monarch?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja Chola II was the son of Kulottunga Chola II (and grandson of Vikrama Chola), continuing the dynasty's temple-building tradition.

questions.q: Darasuram, the temple's location, was historically also known by which royal-sounding name?
questions.explanation: The settlement was known as Rajarajapuram (or Palaiyarai-area) during the Chola era, linked to royal patronage.

questions.q: A notable carved feature of the temple shows a single sculptural panel that can be read as two different animals depending on the angle. What pairing is this?
questions.explanation: A famous composite carving at the temple reads as both an elephant and a bull (rishabha) depending on the viewing angle.

questions.q: The 63 Nayanmars (Shaiva saints) are depicted in a notable series at Darasuram. What were the Nayanmars?
questions.explanation: The Nayanmars were 63 revered devotee-saints of Shiva, and their stories are illustrated in carved panels at the temple.

questions.q: The Airavatesvara Temple represents the later Chola style, contrasting with the colossal scale of which earlier Chola temple?
questions.explanation: While the Brihadisvara Temple at Thanjavur emphasizes grand scale, Darasuram showcases delicate, ornate refinement.

questions.q: The temple's main mandapa, carved as a chariot, is specifically known by which Tamil/Sanskrit term for such a hall?
questions.explanation: The chariot-shaped front hall is known as the Rajagambhiran tirumandapam.

questions.q: Which Tamil Nadu state agency, along with the ASI, is involved in the conservation of such Chola temples?
options: Tamil Nadu Hindu Religious and Charitable Endowments Department
questions.explanation: Living temples like Airavatesvara are administered with the Hindu Religious and Charitable Endowments (HR&CE) Department while ASI handles heritage protection.

questions.q: The temple is famed for stone pillars and miniature carvings so fine they are sometimes compared to work in which other medium?
questions.explanation: The exceptionally delicate miniature stone carvings at Darasuram are often likened to intricate ivory or metal craftsmanship.
